title: carya alba var. subcoriacea
intro: Carya alba var. subcoriacea, also known as the white hickory or shellbark hickory, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its broad, rounded crown and edible nuts. Traditionally, the bark and roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carya alba var. subcoriacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
